dialogos.texto_en: Ah, big bro.
What are you guys up to?
Nothing really,\nwe're just at the beach and relaxing.
The water is crystal clear and continues beyond the horizon.\nThis view never gets boring\nno matter how long you look at it.
Come to think of it, why is the Okinawan ocean\nso beautiful? It looks so different\nfrom the sea at home.
I always had the impression\nthat the sea is darker in color,\nbut the ocean in Okinawa is so transparent.
That's right, it's completely transparent.\nThere is no murkiness at all.
Apparently, there are fewer impurities,\nsuch as plankton, in this area.
It's because of the Kuroshio Current bringing \nin less organic matter at it flows in. That organic\nmatter is the food source for plankton in the sea.
The food source for plankton?\nWhat do you mean?
Well, Kuroshio is the name of the ocean current and\nit carries only a few nutrients.\nWithout nutrients plankton is unable to reproduce.
In other words: if there are fewer impurities from plankton,\nthe sea becomes transparent...\nDo you get it now?
Yeah, I kind of get it.\nAs expected, big bro. You know a lot.
Also, apparently corals are another reason.
Corals?
This area has a lot of corals.\nThe seawater is cleaned by the purifying effect of the coral.
And after the coral dies,\nit turns into white sand on the seabed,\nwhich apparently reflects the sunlight.
Is that so? I didn't know that \ncorals are responsible for the white sand.
Ichika, you know your stuff.\nDid you look that up?
No, actually, the instructor\nwho just lent me the float told me that.
I just heard it so I remembered it.\nNot because I knew it beforehand.
But you remembered it because you were interested,\nright? You wouldn't remember something you're not interested \nin, even if someone shared this info with you.
Ahaha!\nAs usual, you're good at giving compliments, Sensei.\nYou know how to lift people's mood.
I'm not your teacher anymore, you know.\nYour home tutoring is over.
I know that.\nI was just teasing you.
Futaro-kun, you're too nitpicky.\nGuys like that won't get popular, you know?
Though, it's not a problem even if you're unpopular.\nWith Nino by your side, huh.
But, it'll be a problem if Nino-san starts hating you!
It's okay, Nino's love won't cool off that easily.\nAs her older sister, I guarantee it.
Just stop making things up.
Anyway, you were a good teacher.\nThanks to you, I was able to graduate.
That's the greatest reward a teacher could ask for,\nto hear a student say that.
Guess it's a secret talent of mine.\nI learned it because of you guys.
Hmm?\nI think it's rather a talent in development, isn't it?
Do you really think you have the natural talent to teach other\npeople?
I wonder if your talent would work\nwith someone else?
I mean, I wouldn't just praise someone\nwithout knowing them well.
If you don't know the person you're talking to it's just \nflattery, right?
Holy moly.\nYou're taking this way too seriously. Don't be such a dork.
What?!
Anyway, you were a good teacher.\nThanks to you, I was able to graduate.
That's the greatest reward a teacher could ask for,\nto hear a student say that.
I'm tired of being a teacher.\nIt cuts into my study time, and unexpected things\nkeep happening that I can't handle.
Oh, really?\nI always had the impression that you liked it...?
Well, yeah, I guess when I look back on it,\nit was a job worth doing.
And you've also met Nino-san.
W-well, that's true, yeah...
Anyway, you were a good teacher.\nThanks to you, I was able to graduate.
That's the greatest reward a teacher could ask for,\nto hear a student say that.
Still, it's true that you're some kind of maniac\nwho lays his hands on students, though, right?
WOAH!
Hey, you could say it\na little better than that!\nA maniac who lays his hands on students?!
Isn't that true?
Well, it's not wrong!\nBut it makes me sound like a criminal!
But, really, I'm grateful that you helped us Futaro-kun.
You said you wanted to enjoy your high school life,\ndidn't you?
...You said it first though.
Yeah, but...\nI didn't expect we could've\nsuch fulfilling high school days.
Thanks to you,\nwe were able to experience something irreplaceable.
Thank you, Futaro-kun.
No, no, it's weird being praised like that...\nBut, you're welcome.
I never thought that someone would praise you like this\nbig bro...!
Wait, Raiha.\nDo you think I'm a dork?
You're a very good student, who has very few friends and you \ncan barely communicate. Big bro, YOU are a dork.
...Well, I'll admit to having few friends.
You admit that, do you.
Zip it!\nAnyway, I still have some friends, you know?!
Futaro-kun, you're always the same,\nno matter what. I like that about you.
